overview.summary: A sophisticated cyber attack targeting a water treatment facility, involving multiple stages including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access via phishing, malware execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and ultimately impacting SCADA-controlled systems.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of critical infrastructure and potential environmental harm.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather open-source intelligence on the target's water and wastewater systems to identify potential vulnerabilities and entry points.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers develop custom malware tailored for attacking SCADA systems used in water treatment facilities.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Delivering a phishing email containing a malicious attachment or link to gain initial access to the target network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Executing the payload (e.g., SampleCheck5000) to establish a foothold in the target network.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Creating a new user account with elevated privileges to maintain persistent access.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Exploiting vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to escalate privileges within the network.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Deploying rootkits to hide malicious activities and persist across reboots.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Extracting credentials from memory or other sources to gain unauthorized access to critical systems.

attack_flow.stage: Network Mapping <surface/Internal Network Network-Based N/A CAPEC ID: 309 Mapping the target network to identify key assets and potential attack vectors.
Pass-the-Hash <surface/Internal Network <vector/Internal Network N/A CAPEC ID: 461 Using captured hashes to move laterally between systems without needing plaintext credentials.
Data Scraping <surface/Industrial Control Systems <vector/Application N/A CAPEC ID: 592 Collecting sensitive data from SCADA systems related to water treatment processes.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Exfiltrating collected data using encrypted channels to avoid detection.

attack_flow.stage: SCADA Protocol Tampering <surface/Industrial Control Systems <vector/Application N/A CAPEC ID: 328 Modifying data within SCADA communications to disrupt water treatment processes.

Scenario.summary: The attack begins with reconnaissance and resource development, followed by a phishing campaign to gain initial access. The attackers execute malware, establish persistence, escalate privileges, evade defenses, dump credentials, discover network assets, move laterally, collect data, exfiltrate it, and finally impact SCADA-controlled systems to disrupt water treatment operations.
